epigraph.quote: A sword is only feared if it cuts clean. So is a voice — only revered if it is heard and not doubted.

blocks.text: INT. THEATRE — DAY
A projector. Two clips side by side. Mumbled indie film versus Sean Connery. Bond. James Bond.
BROTHER JACK
You don't need Shakespeare diction. You need warrior clarity.
a beat
A mother whispering — 'He's gone' — must pierce like a scream.
A boy whispering — 'I'm not afraid' — must shake the heavens.
If the words float, we never feel their weight.
a beat
Diction isn't the enemy of truth. It's the sword that carries truth.
INT. THEATRE — NIGHT
Morgan Freeman. David Attenborough. James Earl Jones. Alan Rickman. One word. Always. The theatre shivers.
BROTHER JACK
He said one word. And the whole world wept.
a beat
Because grief sat in his throat. He never pushed it. He let it haunt him. Then he gave it to us.
a long beat
You are not here to sound like Morgan. Or Attenborough. Or Mufasa.
You are here to remember why the earth stopped when they spoke.
a beat
Now speak. But only if your breath has something sacred to carry.
INT. THEATRE — NIGHT
Total darkness. One candle. The Apprentice barefoot at centre stage. Eyes closed.
BROTHER JACK
Sixty seconds. No gestures. No expressions. Let the voice carry it all.
Nobility. Urgency. And soul.
She speaks. Her voice cracks — then opens.
BROTHER JACK
very quietly
The greats do not speak to be heard.
They speak to be etched. Like scripture. Like eulogies. Like a name carved into stone.
That — is voice.

The voice lesson is not about sounding good. It is about the words arriving with their full weight intact. Practice the four temperatures — rush, float, cut, oath — daily. Notice which one you default to and why.

requirement.prompt: Post a 60-second video. Voice only. No face. Carry something real with only your voice. A memory, a truth, a line. Let the voice be the entire instrument. Include #JoelhoodP3L3.
